stack_astral_place: Place a non-Astral card to the left
card_8_lore: The Fire Dragon is the eldest of the elemental drakes. Born in the crater of the Kael'ar volcano, it is the living embodiment of creative destruction. Civilizations that faced it are gone. Those that learned to respect it prospered — for a time.
card_8_quote: I don't need a reason to burn.
card_9_lore: No one knows where Thunder Dragons are born. They appear in storms and vanish with them. Some believe they are the storms themselves, given form for a fleeting moment.
card_9_quote: I am not in the storm. I am the storm.
card_10_lore: The Earth Dragon does not move — it moves the earth around it. Each step it takes cracks the ground beneath. Patient as a mountain, relentless as a glacier, and just as impossible to stop.
card_10_quote: Stone does not bend. It crushes.
card_11_lore: The Water Dragon has dwelled in the depths of the Silent Ocean for millennia. It only surfaces when it senses an imbalance in the elements. Its presence always foretells a storm — literally and figuratively.
card_11_quote: The ocean has memory. And it never forgets.
card_12_lore: The Shadow Dragon is the only dragon that leaves no trace. No footprints, no ashes, no witnesses. Historians still debate its existence — but the few survivors of its attacks no longer debate anything.
card_12_quote: What you cannot see kills you better.
card_13_lore: The Light Dragon does not attack — it judges. It scrutinizes every opponent with its golden eyes and decides whether their presence on the battlefield is deserved. Most of the time, its verdict is no.
card_13_quote: Light has no enemy. Only absences.
card_14_lore: Nyolung belongs to no element. It has absorbed them all, digested them all, transcended them all. Other dragons respect it. Elementals fear it. It is the eldest — not because it is the oldest, but because it is what all dragons aspire to become.
card_14_quote: I do not choose my element. I am all the elements.
card_15_lore: Ilidius is not an angel — he is the verdict. Before each battle, he silently evaluates his opponents. Those who acted out of fear or coercion, he spares. Those who chose evil of their own free will, he judges. He has never given a lenient verdict twice in a row.
card_15_quote: The sky does not forgive. It waits.
card_16_lore: Dénotrus was once a knight of light. One of the greatest. Then he stared into the abyss to defeat what he fought — and the abyss stared back. He still wears his armor, but it is black as night. He remembers what he was. It changes nothing.
card_16_quote: I chose power. Power chose me in return.
card_17_lore: The Fire Elemental is not a creature — it is a will. A flame so ancient it has developed a consciousness, so burning it has forgotten what it means to be cold. It does not fight out of hatred. It fights because it is its nature: to consume.
card_17_quote: I do not burn. I am the burning.
card_18_lore: Born in the depths of a bottomless lake, the Water Elemental is the embodiment of patience. It never hastens, never yields to panic. It knows that water always finds its way — and erodes whatever stands in its path.
card_18_quote: Water does not hurry. It always arrives.
card_19_lore: Lyoness left Liotnar the day she understood that light does not belong to a city — it belongs to all who need it. Since then, she wanders from kingdom to kingdom, offering her blade and her protection to those who deserve it. She has no master. She has a cause.
card_19_quote: Light does not ask for permission.
card_20_lore: Adala never learned magic. She stole it from the storm. As a child, she survived a direct lightning strike in the Keth forest — since then, lightning obeys her. Other shamans call her unpredictable. She prefers uncontrollable.
card_20_quote: Lightning never misses the same target twice.
card_21_lore: The fishermen of the Arvel coast know Globmog's story well. Every storm that destroys the docks, every sunken boat, every overturned lighthouse: that's him. He has no particular motivation. He just hates whatever is dry.
card_22_lore: Djormund survived the destruction of his clan, his kingdom, and his world. With each loss, he grew stronger. Not through magic — through refusal. He refuses to die before avenging every one of those he has lost. The universe still owes him much.
card_22_quote: They all fell before me. I am their testimony.
card_23_lore: No one created the Massif Golem — it has existed as long as the mountains have. It does not speak, does not think, obeys no one. It walks. And when it stops, it is because there is nothing left before it.
card_24_lore: The Blood Mage has no name. He had a name, once, but he sacrificed it as he sacrificed everything else — to gain the power to manipulate life itself. He does not fight to win. He fights to feed.
card_24_quote: Your blood is more useful in me than in you.
card_25_lore: No one really knows where this goblin comes from. He appeared one day in a burning village, laughing hysterically amid the flames. He runs from one battlefield to the next, not out of conviction, but because fires amuse him. No use giving him orders — he will do what he wants anyway.
card_26_lore: Eldrich did not learn magic — he understood it. He spent his life studying the space between elements, where no mage had ever looked. What he found in those interstices changed his form, his nature, and his definition of what is possible. Today he is as much an idea as a being.
card_26_quote: The elements are words. I speak the whole language.
card_27_lore: Arthurios has defended Liotnar against three invasions, two natural disasters, and a god. He has never taken a step back. Not out of pride — by choice. He knows that the day he retreats, someone behind him will retreat too. And that someone may be the last line of defense.
card_27_quote: Liotnar does not fall. Not while I stand.
card_28_lore: The Knight of Liotnar does not fight for glory. He fights because he made an oath — and knights of Liotnar do not break their oaths. Even in the face of death. Especially in the face of death.
card_28_quote: My shield holds. Even when my legs no longer do.
card_29_lore: He has no battle name yet. No scars yet. No legends yet. But he has something many warriors have lost through fighting: the conviction that it is worth it. The Squire of Liotnar is not the strongest on this field. He may be the last standing, precisely because he does not yet know he should be afraid.
card_29_quote: I'm not ready. But I'm here.
tuto_text_goal:
Destroy all of your opponent's cards to win the match.
Each player starts with 5 fixed cards on the board.
tuto_text_turn:
You have 2 actions per turn. Each action lets you use a skill from one of your cards.
You must end your turn manually. A 2-minute timer applies — if time runs out, the turn passes automatically.
tuto_text_cards:
Click on one of your cards to view its skills. Each card has 2 skills and a unique passive ability.
Some skills have a cooldown: wait the indicated number of turns before using them again.
Click on an enemy card to inspect its skills (read-only).
tuto_text_skills:
Attack — deals damage to an enemy target.
Heal — restores HP to an allied target.
Buff — strengthens one or more allied cards.
Debuff — weakens one or more enemy cards.
Mixed — a combination of offensive and defensive effects.
tuto_text_elements:
7 elements: Fire, Water, Earth, Air, Light, Shadow and Astral (max 10 stacks each).
Each action generates stacks for your team.
Minor bonuses: active from the 1st stack, benefit all your cards.
Major bonuses: activate at 3 or 5 stacks for cards of the same element.
tuto_text_armor:
Armor is an additional HP pool (max 100).
Damage is applied to armor first, then to HP once armor is depleted.
Some skills pierce armor and directly attack HP.
tuto_text_passive:
Each card has a unique passive that triggers under specific conditions:
turn start, card destruction, stack threshold reached...
Inspect enemy cards to anticipate their passives.
